Test Description: Validate GPIO register default values and write/read behavior for a defined register set using masks and deterministic data patterns.
Remarks: VRRW registers are skipped as marked by skip_array[]; Some reset checks are skipped as marked by skip_rst_array[]; Note: when reading default values the DIN value becomes 1 automatically if not forced; forcing zero can set bit-level select high causing mismatches.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and setup:
- Execute the test entry routine for this case.

Initialization:
- Initialize failure counters to zero.

Default value check (per register, in order):
- For each index from 0 to CNT-1, select the address from addr_array[].
- If the corresponding skip_rst_array[] entry equals 1, skip this address for reset-value reading.
- If the corresponding read_mask_array[] entry equals 0x00000000, skip (not readable).
- Read the register at the selected address; apply mask 0xFFFFFFFE to the read data.
- Compare the masked read value against default_value_array[] for that index.
- On mismatch, increment the default-value failure counter and log address, expected, and read values.

Write and read verification (per test pattern):
- Iterate across the fixed data patterns in the exact order: 0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000.
- For each pattern, perform two passes across all indices 0..CNT-1:
  - Write pass:
    - For each index: obtain the address from addr_array[].
    - If skip_array[] entry equals 1, skip this address.
    - If write_mask_array[] entry equals 0x00000000, skip (not writable).
    - Otherwise, write the value (pattern AND write_mask_array[]) to the register at the selected address.
  - Read/verify pass:
    - For each index: obtain the address from addr_array[].
    - Skip if any of these conditions hold: skip_array[]=1, write_mask_array[]=0x00000000, or read_mask_array[]=0x00000000.
    - Read the register at the address and apply read_mask_array[] to the read value.
    - Compute the expected value as: (pattern AND read_mask_array[] AND write_mask_array[]) OR ((bitwise NOT of write_mask_array[]) AND read_mask_array[] AND default_value_array[]).
    - Compare the masked read value against the computed expected value; on mismatch, increment the write/read failure counter and log address, expected, and read values.

Completion:
- If any failure counters are non-zero, mark the test as failed; otherwise, mark the test as passed.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass when: (a) All readable registers’ default values (with bit0 masked) equal their entries in default_value_array[]; (b) For each data pattern, all readable/writable registers return the computed expected value under the defined masks; (c) Final failure counters are zero. Otherwise, fail.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests; Some register addresses are intentionally skipped via skip arrays; Soft-reset path is present but disabled; No explicit verification of side-effect semantics beyond provided masks; No concurrency or timing stress applied.

Test Description: Enable and verify negedge-triggered interrupts for GPIOs 8–39, including per-pin configuration, group enable, raw status clear, ISR handling, and system interrupt clear using a driven input source.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and setup:
- If GPIO0 is defined, enable interrupt line 87 in the interrupt controller; if GPIO1 is defined, enable line 88.
- Enable the corresponding system-register interrupt by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with the appropriate GPIO0/GPIO1 interrupt enable bit.

Initialization and configuration:
- Drive the external data source at address 0xA0243ffc to 0xFFFFFFFF.
- For each pin index 0..31 (GPIOs 8–39): program the per-pin control register at base MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (index × 4) with the value (1<<20) | (1<<18) | (1<<16).

Per-pin test loop and trigger:
- For each pin index 0..31:
  - Compute the bit mask wr_val = 1 shifted left by the index.
  - Clear any pending raw status by writing wr_val to MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1.
  - Enable the group interrupt for this pin by writing wr_val to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1; wait for 10 units.
  - Arm the pending flag by setting int_pend to 1.
  - Ensure the external source is high by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xA0243ffc; wait for 30 units.
  - Create a falling edge for the selected pin by writing the bitwise NOT of wr_val to 0xA0243ffc.
  - Wait until the interrupt is serviced (int_pend cleared) with a timeout of 5000 iterations, each delaying 10 units; on timeout, record an error for the corresponding GPIO (index + 8).

Runtime handling (ISR behavior on interrupt):
- Immediately set int_pend to 0 to signal service start.
- Drive 0xA0243ffc to 0xFFFFFFFF (quiesce input source).
- Read the per-pin control/status register at MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (current index × 4) into rdata.
- Verify input-status bit0 is 0; on non-zero, record an error.
- Read group status at MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 and confirm the local pin bit is set; on mismatch, record an error.
- Acknowledge per-pin by writing (1<<20) | (1<<16) to the same per-pin register.
- Clear raw status for this pin by writing the local bit to MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1.
- Re-read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 and verify it is 0x0; on non-zero, record an error.
- Clear the system raw status by writing the appropriate GPIO0/GPIO1 bit to MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, then clear the corresponding GIC interrupt (87 or 88).

Completion:
- Report overall pass if no timeouts or verification errors were recorded; otherwise fail.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass when for each GPIO 8–39: (a) An interrupt is observed within the timeout after generating a falling edge; (b) Group status reflects the pin’s bit during service; (c) Per-pin acknowledge and raw-status clear return group status to 0x0; (d) System-level raw status is cleared; (e) No unexpected input or status bits are set; Overall test_err is zero.
Gap Analysis: No debounce or glitch filtering checks; No simultaneous or back-to-back interrupt stress; Dependence on int_pend software flag; No negative/error-injection paths.

Test Description: Enable posedge-triggered interrupts on all GPIOs 8–39, exercise one rising-edge event per pin using an external driver, and validate group status, raw clear, system interrupt clear, and mask/unmask behavior in the ISR.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and setup:
- If GPIO0 is defined, enable interrupt line 87; if GPIO1 is defined, enable line 88.
- Enable the corresponding system-register interrupt by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with the appropriate GPIO0/GPIO1 interrupt enable bit.

Initialization and configuration:
- For each pin index 0..31: enable posedge detection by writing 0x00020000 to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (index × 4).
- Wait 10 units.
- Configure GPIOs 8–39 for input by writing 0x000000FF to each of MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, and MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4.
- Wait 10 units.
- Enable group interrupts by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1.

Per-pin trigger and wait:
- For each pin index 0..31:
  - Drive the external source at 0xA0243ffc low with 0x00000000; wait 10 units.
  - Arm by setting int_pend to 1.
  - Drive the external source high with 0xFFFFFFFF to generate a rising edge.
  - Wait until the interrupt is serviced (int_pend cleared) with a timeout of 2000 iterations, delaying 10 units each; on timeout, record an error and stop further iterations.
  - Optionally drive low again (0x00000000) and wait 10 units to prepare for the next iteration.

Runtime handling (ISR behavior on interrupt):
- Compute the current pin bit as (1 << i); set int_pend to 0.
- Read group status at MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1; temporarily mask group output by writing 0x00000000 to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 during service.
- If group status is non-zero, proceed; otherwise record an error.
- Clear per-pin raw status across all pins by writing 0x00010000 to each per-pin register MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j × 4) for j = 0..31; wait 2 units.
- Re-read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 and verify 0x0; on non-zero, record an error.
- Clear the system raw status by writing the appropriate GPIO0/GPIO1 bit to MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1; read back and ensure the bit is cleared; re-enable group interrupts by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1; clear the relevant GIC interrupt (87 or 88).

Completion:
- Report overall pass if no timeouts or verification errors were recorded; otherwise fail.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass when: (a) Each GPIO 8–39 generates a posedge interrupt observed within the timeout; (b) Group status is asserted during service and returns to 0x0 after raw clear; (c) System-level raw status is cleared and readback shows deasserted; (d) Overall test_err remains zero.
Gap Analysis: No per-pin functional readback beyond group status; No debounce/noise resilience checks; No simultaneous multi-pin assertions; Limited timing coverage; Dependence on software flag for completion.
